# PacificaRomania

> PacificaRomania is a cultural art collection of over 150 works of Pacific and Island Southeast Asian art — painted Dreamings, war canoes, carved ancestors, and sacred vessels — first exhibited at the Embassy of Romania in Canberra (2019–2020) and read through the comparative history of religion of Mircea Eliade and the sculptural cosmology of Constantin Brâncuși.

Operated by AlgorithmIntelligence SRL, Bucharest, Romania. Contact: danbarbu22@gmail.com. All pages are bilingual (English and Romanian, both languages present in the same HTML).

## Collection

- [Collection](https://pacificaromania.space/collection.html): Over 150 works of Pacific and Island Southeast Asian art, first exhibited at the Embassy of Romania in Canberra (2019–2020). Browse by region: Australia, Melanesia, Micronesia, Polynesia, and Island Southeast Asia.
- [Australia](https://pacificaromania.space/collection/australia.html): Australia: Dreaming, Desert Law & the Longest Living Tradition. Objects from the PacificaRomania collection.
- [Island Southeast Asia](https://pacificaromania.space/collection/island-southeast-asia.html): Dayak baby carriers, Batak medicine vessels, Burmese weights, and the shamanic masks of the Malaysian Orang Asli.
- [Melanesia](https://pacificaromania.space/collection/melanesia.html): Melanesia: The Great Engine of Ritual Invention. Objects from the PacificaRomania collection.
- [Micronesia](https://pacificaromania.space/collection/micronesia.html): Micronesia: A Cartographic Metaphysics of the Atoll. Objects from the PacificaRomania collection.
- [Polynesia](https://pacificaromania.space/collection/polynesia.html): Polynesia: Art as Genealogical Inscription. Objects from the PacificaRomania collection.

## Journal (curatorial essays)

- [Journal](https://pacificaromania.space/journal.html): Curatorial essays from the PacificaRomania collection, connecting Pacific and Southeast Asian art to Mircea Eliade's comparative history of religion.
- [Aboriginal Birds in Space](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/aboriginal-birds-in-space.html): A preface to the installation: Seven Sisters Dreaming, Arnhem Land poles, and Brâncuși's ascent.
- [The “Picasso of the Pacific”](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/aloi-pilioko.html): Aloï Pilioko in the PacificaRomania collection.
- [Living Ancestors](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/asaro-mudmen.html): The Asaro Mudmen legend and the Eastern Highlands spirit mask.
- [The First Canvas: Body Paint](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/body-paint.html): The ephemeral designs of women’s ceremony - painted on skin, danced, and rubbed away - transposed to a permanent surface.
- [The Law of the Harvest: Bush Banana](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/bush-banana.html): The life cycle of the silky pear turned into a blossoming field of colour - ecological law dressed as celebration.
- [The Healing Whirlwind: Bush Medicine Leaves](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/bush-medicine-leaves.html): A single motif - the leaves of the medicine plant - set swirling across the canvas until pharmacopoeia becomes choreography.
- [The Land as Anchor: Central Australian Landscape](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/central-australian-landscape.html): The ranges of the central desert rendered with watercolour precision - and read as the very ground of identity and belonging.
- [The Worn Crown: The Dhari](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/dhari-headdress.html): The ceremonial headdress at the centre of the Torres Strait Islander flag - an emblem of a distinct, seafaring, Melanesian identity.
- [Dreaming the Sacred: Eliade & the Masks of Malaysia](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/eliade-and-the-masks-of-malaysia.html): How Mah Meri ritual shaped a Romanian historian of religion.
- [Keeper of the Boundary: Freshwater Crocodile](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/freshwater-crocodile.html): On one of the world’s oldest continuous artistic surfaces, fine cross-hatching maps a water cosmology across the crocodile’s body.
- [Two Dreamings, One Field: Frog and Kangaroo](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/frog-and-kangaroo-dreaming.html): A founding master of the Western Desert lays two ancestral estates over one canvas - the frog of rain and renewal, the kangaroo of the travelling track.
- [Witnesses, Not Scenery: Ghost Gums](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/ghost-gums.html): A watercolour that looks like landscape and reads like ontology - the ghost gums standing not as scenery but as living ancestral presences.
- [The Vertical Return: Larrakitj](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/larrakitj-ezariah-kelly.html): A hollow tree trunk, painted with sacred clan designs, that once ferried the bones of the dead back into ancestral Country.
- [The Second Pole: Larrakitj](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/larrakitj-napunda-marawili.html): A companion memorial pole whose clan designs map the deceased’s Country and dissolve a single life back into the collective.
- [The Topography of a Force: Mountain Devil Lizard](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/mountain-devil-lizard.html): Immensely fine dotting tracks not a place but a power - the micro-movements of the ancestral Arnkerrthe across her Country.
- [Moyang Siamang — The Primate Ancestor Spirit](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/moyang-siamang.html): A carved cosmology at the edge of three worlds: the sacred primate through Orang Asli, Chinese, and Indian eyes.
- [The Power of the Vessel](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/naga-morsarang.html): Batak medicine containers, from empty horn to complete cosmogram.
- [Desire as Law: The Ngarlu Love Story](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/ngarlu-love-story.html): At the site of Ngarlu, an ancestral man is drawn into forbidden desire - and Clifford Possum draws it with the restraint of a diagram.
- [The Spirit of the Tomoko](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/nguzu-nguzu-and-the-tomoko.html): Maritime ritual and cultural resilience in the Solomon Islands.
- [The First Teachers and the World-Maker](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/nine-mimis-and-rainbow-serpent.html): The thin Mimi spirits - who taught the first people to hunt, paint and dance - gather before the great ancestral serpent of creation.
- [The Raja Bomoh and the Great Bird](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/raja-bomoh.html): A Malay shaman, a missing aeroplane, and a contemporary sculpture that deifies the bomoh.
- [The Pursued Constellation: Seven Sisters](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/seven-sisters.html): The Pleiades flee across the night sky and across the ground below at once - a star-chart and a land-map folded together.
- [The Unresolved Frame: The Missionaries](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/the-missionaries.html): Ancestral figures crowned with crosses - two faiths held in unresolved tension within a single canvas.
- [The Sacralization of Measure](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/the-sacralization-of-measure.html): Burmese weights in the PacificaRomania collection.
- [Spiritual Armor: The Sacred Papot](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/the-sacred-papot.html): Dayak baby carriers as portable sacred centres.
- [The Transmutation of the Sovereign: From the Carpathian Vampire to the Malayan Tiger in Chains](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/tiger-in-chains.html): Vlad the Impaler, the Fanged King of Kedah, and the Mah Meri 'Tiger in Chains': how cinema tamed two monstrous sovereigns, and the indigenous carving that resists.
- [The Compressed Archive: Tingari](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/tingari.html): Disciplined geometry compresses geography, ancestry, ceremony and law into a single lattice - and keeps the restricted core invisible.
- [Country as Incandescence: Waltitjata](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/waltitjata.html): A Pitjantjatjara elder dissolves the desert’s map into pure colour - sacred geography experienced as light.
- [What Is Our Relationship with Water?](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/water-dreaming.html): A topography not of where the water is, but of what the water is - ancestry, memory and responsibility beneath the desert.
- [The Living Track: Water Goanna](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/water-goanna.html): A guide through Country carried from the ancient Dreaming into a contemporary, partly urban present.
- [Two Worlds, One Horizon](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/witch-doctor-and-the-windmill.html): The traditional healer stands beside the windmill of the mission - and the painting refuses to choose between them.
- [The Sweetness Underground: Honey Ant Dreaming](https://pacificaromania.space/journal/yuelamu-honey-ant-dreaming.html): A map of the tunnels and chambers where the honey ant stores its nectar - women’s ecological knowledge rendered as ethnoscience.

## About

- [About](https://pacificaromania.space/about.html): PacificaRomania is a private collection and curatorial project reading Pacific and Southeast Asian art through the comparative lens of Mircea Eliade and Constantin Brâncuși.
- [Contact](https://pacificaromania.space/contact.html): Get in touch with the PacificaRomania collection.
- [PacificaRomania — A Cultural Art Collection Between Romania and the Pacific](https://pacificaromania.space/): PacificaRomania is a cultural art collection exploring the living cosmologies of Aboriginal Australia, Melanesia, Micronesia, Polynesia and Island Southeast Asia, read through the comparative lens of Mircea Eliade and Constantin Brâncuși.
